title: Incidence Rate Analysis
description: Incidence Rate analysis estimates the rate of a new outcome event in a target population, optionally stratified by age, sex, and calendar year. It measures how often an outcome occurs per 100 person-years in the at-risk period.
tips: The target cohort defines the at-risk population; the outcome cohort defines the event being measured.
An outcome cohort should typically use 'First Event' to avoid counting re-occurrences.
Clean windows (time-at-risk start/end) define the exact period during which patients are observed for the outcome.
Stratified results by age/sex are available in the Breakdown tab post-execution.
Use multiple outcome cohorts in one analysis to compare incidence across related outcomes efficiently.
